gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Laptop GPU (16 GB, CC 12.0)
model: /tmp/eliza1-eval-models/Qwen3-0.6B-Q8_0.gguf  (Qwen3-0.6B-Q8_0 — stands in for the 0_6b text backbone in pooled-text mode and for the Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B dedicated region; the dedicated embedding GGUF is the same 0.6B lineage)

cpu.note: Quiescent host (no other llama-server running); 24 threads, Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX.

cpu_contended.note: Same config but with the dflash chat llama-server competing for cores — shows throughput is ~2x lower under CPU contention; left in to document the realistic shared-host case.

vulkan.note: RADV/AMD path is N/A on this box (RTX 5080) — Vulkan here runs on the NVIDIA Vulkan ICD; numbers track CUDA closely on single-text, fall behind on big batches because the Vulkan backend doesn't fuse the embedding-pooling matmuls as well as cuBLAS.

cuda.note: RTX 5080 Laptop, n-gpu-layers 99. Cold-load is dominated by CUDA context + cuBLAS warmup (~21 s first launch; the runtime amortizes this since the sidecar is started once per bundle and reused). Best throughput: ~684 texts/s at batch 16.

comparison_pooled_text_vs_dedicated.summary: On 0_6b the embedding model IS the text backbone with --pooling last — no separate weights, no extra cold-load (the chat server already mmapped the file; the embedding sidecar shares the OS page cache). On 1_7b+ the dedicated embedding/eliza-1-embedding.gguf is Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B (same 0.6B lineage, ~600 MB Q8_0), acquired lazily on first embed(). Quality: the dedicated model carries Qwen's contrastive embedding head and is the one trained for the 1024-dim Matryoshka contract — pooled-text last-token states from a raw base/instruct model are usable for short-text similarity but were never calibrated for the truncated widths, so on the larger tiers we MUST use the dedicated region (AGENTS.md §1; B1's verdict). Speed: identical (same 0.6B arch, same llama-server flags); the only difference is the extra ~600 MB to page in on first embed() (lazy — a voice-off / RAG-off agent never pays it).
comparison_pooled_text_vs_dedicated.matryoshka_caveat: The pairwise-ranking-pearson numbers above were measured on the Qwen3-0.6B base GGUF (pooled-text proxy). The dedicated Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B is *trained* for these truncation points, so its real numbers at 64/128/256 should be at or above these — the table is a conservative lower bound.

methodology: embedding_bench.mjs drives a llama-server --embeddings --pooling last; single-text latency = median over 24 runs after a warmup; throughput = batch / wall over >=3 reps per batch size; coldLoadMs = spawn -> /health; peakRssMb = max of /proc/<pid>/statm RSS sampled every 200 ms; pairwise_ranking_pearson_vs_full = Pearson correlation between the 16-sentence corpus's pairwise-cosine matrix at the truncated width and at 1024 — an offline retrieval-ranking-preservation proxy when MTEB isn't available.
